title: Episode 2 — Iran War Justification (v12)
description: The Foundry Council debates whether the United States was justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran. Mercer (the realist) argues yes — a regime weeks from breakout, with the inspectors locked out, cannot be allowed legal patience. Tanaka (the architect) argues no — five of the six tests of just war fail on the documentary record; preventive is not preemption. Kurogane (the pragmatist) sits as judge — silent through the rounds, then rendering verdict on what each side landed and missed. v12 doctrine: head-to-head debate + silent judge structure, every fact paired with sensory anchor (mirror neurons rule), causal turns quoting prior speaker's exact phrase, escalating stakes across rounds, setup-payoff threading, three-sentence Juliet outro with no recap and no jury framing. v10 + v11 archived.
summary: Was the United States justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran? Mercer for, Tanaka against, Kurogane judges. v12 doctrine — head-to-head + silent judge.
